--- title: "JSC370 Final Project" author: "Your Name" jupyter: python3 execute: warning: false message: false echo: false --- This is my JSC370 Final Project website. I will showcase a few interactive visuals here. ## First step First I will load and process the Starbucks data: ::: {#27bc3fc0 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import plotly.express as px import plotly.graph_objects as go from collections import Counter import re # Load data sb_locs = pd.read_csv("starbucks-locations.csv") sb_nutr = pd.read_csv("starbucks-menu-nutrition.csv", skipinitialspace=True) usa_pop = pd.read_csv("us_state_pop.csv") usa_states = pd.read_csv("states.csv") # Filter US locations and count stores per state sb_usa = sb_locs[sb_locs["Country"] == "US"] sb_locs_state = ( sb_usa .groupby("State/Province") .size() .reset_index(name="n_stores") .rename(columns={"State/Province": "state"}) ) # Merge with population (via state abbreviation) usa_pop_abbr = usa_pop.merge(usa_states, left_on="state", right_on="State") sb_locs_state = sb_locs_state.merge( usa_pop_abbr, left_on="state", right_on="Abbreviation" ) # Top 10 words in menu item names stop_words = {"the", "and", "with", "a", "an", "of", "in", "or", "no", "on"} word_counts = Counter( w for name in sb_nutr["Item"].dropna() for w in re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z]+", name.lower()) if len(w) > 2 and w not in stop_words ) top10_words = [w for w, _ in word_counts.most_common(10)] def find_top_word(item_name): for w in top10_words: if w in str(item_name).lower(): return w return None sb_nutr["top_word"] = sb_nutr["Item"].apply(find_top_word) sb_top = sb_nutr.dropna(subset=["top_word"]).copy() ``` :::
## Second step Then I will add some code to create the **plotly** figures. Note: Name each code chunk, like this one: `plot1` ::: {#cell-plot1 .cell execution_count=2} ::: {#plot1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Third step Create tabs to display each figure. ::: {.panel-tabset} ### Histogram of Calories ::: {#cell-p1 .cell execution_count=3} ::: {#p1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Boxplot of Nutrition ::: {#cell-p2 .cell execution_count=4} ::: {#p2 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: Done!
